# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Network Container Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network/commits)


Terraform module for provisioning MongoDB Atlas Network Containers, providing the foundational
network isolation layer for Atlas clusters. Supports AWS, GCP, and Azure cloud providers with a
unified interface. Designed for hub-and-spoke network architectures and follows the CloudOps Works
standard organization/environment variable convention.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module creates a [MongoDB Atlas Network Container](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2/#tag/Network-Peering/operation/createPeeringContainer)
— the prerequisite resource for setting up VPC/VNet peering between Atlas clusters and your cloud provider network.

**Key capabilities:**

- **Multi-cloud support**: A single module interface handles AWS, GCP, and AZURE network containers
  via the `settings.provider` attribute.
- **Project resolution**: Supply either a `project_id` directly or a `project_name` for automatic
  lookup — the module resolves the correct Atlas project ID either way.
- **Hub/spoke aware**: The `is_hub` and `spoke_def` variables integrate with the CloudOps Works
  naming and tagging conventions, generating consistent `system_name` locals used across the stack.
- **Standardised tagging**: Inherits organisation/environment tags from the `cloudopsworks/tags/local`
  module and merges any additional tags supplied via `extra_tags`.

**Outputs** expose all provider-specific container attributes (VPC ID, GCP project/network, Azure
subscription/VNet) so downstream peering modules can reference them directly.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network/releases).


### AWS Example (Terragrunt)

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.git?ref=v1.0.1"
}

inputs = {
  # Hub/spoke topology
  is_hub    = false   # (Optional) Is this a hub or spoke configuration? Default: false
  spoke_def = "001"   # (Optional) Spoke ID Number, must be a 3-digit string. Default: "001"

  # Organisation details
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"       # (Required) The name of the organisation
    organization_unit = "platform"   # (Required) The unit within the organisation
    environment_type  = "production" # (Required) The type of environment (e.g. production, staging)
    environment_name  = "prod"       # (Required) The name of the environment
  }

  # MongoDB Atlas project — supply one of project_id or project_name
  project_id   = ""              # (Optional) Direct Atlas project ID. Overrides project_name. Default: "".
  project_name = "my-atlas-proj" # (Optional) Atlas project name for automatic ID lookup. Default: "".

  # Network container settings
  settings = {
    cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/21" # (Required) IPv4 CIDR block for the Atlas network container.
    provider   = "AWS"          # (Required) Cloud provider: "AWS", "GCP", or "AZURE".
    region     = "us-east-1"   # (Optional) AWS region in lowercase-hyphenated format.
                                #            Module converts to uppercase-underscore for the Atlas API.
                                #            Example: "us-east-1" → API value "US_EAST_1". Default: "us-east-1".
  }

  # Additional resource tags
  extra_tags = {                # (Optional) Extra tags to add to resources. Default: {}
    CostCenter = "engineering"
    Team       = "platform"
  }
}
```

### GCP Example (Terragrunt)

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.git?ref=v1.0.1"
}

inputs = {
  is_hub    = true
  spoke_def = "001"

  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }

  project_name = "my-atlas-proj"

  settings = {
    cidr_block = "10.8.0.0/18"                    # (Required) IPv4 CIDR block for the Atlas network container.
    provider   = "GCP"                             # (Required) Cloud provider: "AWS", "GCP", or "AZURE".
    regions    = ["US_EAST_4", "US_CENTRAL1"]      # (Optional) GCP regions list — uppercase-underscore format.
                                                   #            Passed directly to the Atlas API (no transformation).
  }
}
```

### Azure Example (Terragrunt)

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.git?ref=v1.0.1"
}

inputs = {
  is_hub    = false
  spoke_def = "002"

  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }

  project_id = "64a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1"

  settings = {
    cidr_block = "10.16.0.0/21" # (Required) IPv4 CIDR block for the Atlas network container.
    provider   = "AZURE"         # (Required) Cloud provider: "AWS", "GCP", or "AZURE".
    region     = "us-east-2"    # (Optional) Azure region in lowercase-hyphenated format.
                                 #            Module converts to uppercase-underscore for the Atlas API.
                                 #            Example: "us-east-2" → API value "US_EAST_2". Default: "us-east-2".
  }
}
```

## Quick Start

1. **Add the module** to your Terragrunt stack:

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-network.git?ref=v1.0.1"
}

inputs = {
  org = {
    organization_name = "myorg"
    organization_unit = "ops"
    environment_type  = "dev"
    environment_name  = "dev"
  }
  project_name = "my-atlas-project"
  settings = {
    cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/21"
    provider   = "AWS"
    region     = "us-east-1"
  }
}
```

2. **Run** `terragrunt apply` — the network container is created in your Atlas project.
3. **Reference outputs** in your peering module using `dependency` blocks.


## Examples

See the `usage` section above for AWS, GCP, and Azure Terragrunt examples.

**Outputs available after apply:**

| Output | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `network_container_id` | Atlas network container ID |
| `network_container_provisioned` | Whether the container has been provisioned |
| `network_container_aws_region` | AWS region of the container |
| `network_container_aws_vpc_id` | AWS VPC ID associated with the container |
| `network_container_gcp_project_id` | GCP project ID of the container |
| `network_container_gcp_network_name` | GCP network name of the container |
| `network_container_azure_region` | Azure region of the container |
| `network_container_azure_subscription_id` | Azure subscription ID of the container |
| `network_container_azure_vnet_name` | Azure VNet name of the container |

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_project_id"></a> [project\_id](#input\_project\_id) | (optional) The ID of the project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_project_name"></a> [project\_name](#input\_project\_name) | (optional) The name of the project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | Settings for the module | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
